name: Arthur's Seat
title: A city built on volcanoes
note: Pictures for the big-picture opening: the city on its volcanic crags, and James Hutton, father of geology.

items.caption: Edinburgh spread below the Salisbury Crags. The whole city is built on and around the worn-down roots of ancient volcanoes, scraped bare by Ice Age glaciers.
items.kidcaption: See the whole city from up on the crags! Edinburgh is built on top of old, worn-down volcanoes. Don't worry, they've been asleep for millions of years.

items.caption: James Hutton, the Edinburgh farmer and thinker often called the father of geology, who realised from these rocks that the Earth was almost unimaginably old, an idea called deep time.
items.kidcaption: This man, James Hutton, looked at Edinburgh's rocks and worked out that the Earth is millions and millions of years old, way older than anyone had guessed.

items.caption: Arthur's Seat, a genuine extinct volcano rising wild in the middle of a capital city, one of the strangest and finest city parks anywhere.
items.kidcaption: Arthur's Seat is a real extinct volcano you can climb, right in the middle of the city! How many cities have a mountain in the park?
